title: Chord basics
intro: A friendly, no-pressure explainer. Read as much or as little as you like — then go play.

sections.heading: What is a chord?
sections.body: A chord is a mini team of notes played together to make your guitar sound full and musical. Think of it like several strings joining forces to create one big feeling.

sections.heading: Why do chords exist?
sections.body: Chords help turn simple notes into real music by adding harmony, color, and emotion. They are one of the main reasons songs can feel happy, dreamy, powerful, or a little dramatic.

sections.heading: A tiny bit of history
sections.body: Chords have been around for centuries as music grew from single melodies into richer harmonies. Over time, they became a huge part of styles like folk, blues, rock, pop, and jazz.

sections.heading: Major vs minor
sections.body: Major chords usually sound bright and cheerful, while minor chords often sound softer, moodier, or more emotional. Neither is better — they just tell different musical stories.

sections.heading: Why guitar players love chords
sections.body: Chords let you play songs quickly, support singing, and sound great even as a beginner. With just a few simple shapes, you can already start making real music.

sections.heading: How chords work in Riffly
sections.body: In Riffly, you begin with easy chords like E minor and A minor, then learn how to switch between them smoothly and strum in time. Step by step, those simple shapes grow into real songs, better rhythm, and bigger musical confidence.
